test_cases.title: Reach the Checkout Overview after submitting valid information
test_cases.description: With two items in the cart, proceeding through checkout and submitting a valid first name, last name, and postal code reaches the Checkout: Overview step showing both items.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: This is the multi-step journey that gates the whole checkout: cart -> information form -> overview. It can only be exercised by driving the real two-page form flow and confirming the overview renders both items. No API models this composite navigation. P0 because every other overview assertion depends on reaching this step.
preconditions: Shopper is signed in as standard_user on https://www.saucedemo.com.
Two distinct items (Sauce Labs Backpack, Sauce Labs Bike Light) have been added to the cart.

steps.action: Open the cart and click Checkout.
On the Checkout: Your Information step, fill First Name, Last Name, and Zip/Postal Code with valid values.

expected_results: The Checkout: Overview step is reached.
Both items (Sauce Labs Backpack and Sauce Labs Bike Light) are listed on the overview.

test_cases.title: Order summary Item Total equals the sum of the cart items' unit prices
test_cases.description: On the Checkout: Overview step, the displayed Item Total equals the exact sum of the unit prices of the items in the cart.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Money-math regression-critical case. The test MUST read the actual rendered unit prices of the cart items and the rendered 'Item total' from the overview page and assert their equality by computation — it must NOT hard-code a magic number from the story text (prices can change). Only observable on the rendered overview; SauceDemo exposes no order-summary API.
preconditions: Shopper is on the Checkout: Overview step with Sauce Labs Backpack and Sauce Labs Bike Light in the cart (via TC-001 flow).

steps.action: Read the rendered unit price of each item on the overview (or the cart).
Read the rendered 'Item total: $X.XX' value on the overview.
Compute the sum of the unit prices and compare to the Item total.

expected_results: The displayed Item total equals the sum of the two items' unit prices (read live, not hard-coded).

test_cases.title: Order summary Total equals Item Total plus Tax (internally consistent)
test_cases.description: On the Checkout: Overview step, the displayed Total equals the displayed Item Total plus the displayed Tax.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: The second money-math guarantee: the on-screen arithmetic must add up. The test MUST read the rendered 'Item total', 'Tax', and 'Total' values and assert Total == Item total + Tax by computation (allowing for cent rounding), never a hard-coded figure. Observable only on the rendered overview.
preconditions: Shopper is on the Checkout: Overview step (via TC-001 flow).

steps.action: Read the rendered 'Item total', 'Tax', and 'Total' values from the overview summary.
Assert Total equals Item total + Tax (to the cent).

expected_results: The displayed Total equals the displayed Item total plus the displayed Tax.

test_cases.title: Missing required field keeps the shopper on the Information step with an error
test_cases.description: Submitting the information form with a missing required field (e.g. no postal code) keeps the shopper on the information step and shows an error; they do not advance to the overview.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Negative / validation case: form submission with a missing field must be rejected client-side and NOT advance. Only the rendered error message and the fact that the URL stays on the information step confirm it. This guards RISK-003 (incomplete data proceeding).
preconditions: Shopper is on the Checkout: Your Information step with at least one item in the cart.

steps.action: Fill First Name and Last Name but leave Zip/Postal Code empty.

expected_results: An error message is shown indicating the postal code is required.
The shopper remains on the Checkout: Your Information step (does not reach the overview).

test_cases.title: Finish reaches the confirmation and clears the cart badge
test_cases.description: Clicking Finish on the overview reaches the Checkout: Complete! confirmation and the cart badge is cleared.
test_cases.automation_decision_reason: Completes the journey: Finish -> confirmation, and the cart badge must be cleared (order placed). The confirmation text and the badge absence are only observable in the rendered UI after the real Finish action. Guards RISK-004 (order state ambiguity).
preconditions: Shopper is on the Checkout: Overview step (via TC-001 flow).

steps.action: Click Finish on the overview step.

expected_results: The Checkout: Complete! confirmation page is reached (e.g. a 'Thank you for your order' message).
The cart badge is no longer present (the cart has been cleared).
